metadata.title: Bizarre Lineage Tier List: Best Stands for PvP & PvE
metadata.description: Master the Bizarre Lineage meta with our updated tier list. Compare the best stands for competitive PvP and efficient PvE farming in Morioh.

board.title: Bizarre Lineage Stand Tier List
board.description: The ultimate guide to every Stand in the game. Updated for March 2026 based on community meta research.
board.pro_tip: When deciding whether to keep or skip {standName}, consider your current Prestige level and Conjuring stat. This stand peaks at high Conjuring where its true potential is unlocked.

representative.title: The cards explain why a stand earns attention, not just where it lands.

methodology.title: A useful list states its evaluation frame.

role_picks.title: Some readers only want the best option for a specific situation.

faq.title: What the ranking page should clarify.

items.question: Why link the tier list to a stand page?
items.answer: Because the ranking page should hand off to a database-style article when a stand deserves deeper research.

items.question: Why is methodology so visible here?
items.answer: Without methodology, a tier list becomes a subjective chart that is hard to trust and hard to differentiate in search.

items.question: Should every stand be in the MVP?
items.answer: No. The MVP uses a representative set and one strong sample page before scaling coverage.

items.question: What should new players read after this page?
items.answer: The beginner guide, especially if they understand the meta but still need an early progression route.

entries.name: Star Platinum
entries.summary: Reliable pressure, broad matchup value, and enough versatility to anchor the current sample stand page.

entries.name: King Crimson
entries.summary: High upside, but it asks for cleaner decision-making and punishes sloppy execution more than the top slot.

entries.name: Silver Chariot
entries.summary: Usable and serviceable, though more dependent on comfort, matchup knowledge, and player preference.

entries.name: Entry-path stands
entries.summary: Fine for getting moving, but not the stands most players should structure long-term plans around.

methodology.title: Consistency beats clip value
methodology.description: The ranking favors stands that perform across repeat matches rather than isolated highlight moments.

methodology.title: PvP and PvE both matter
methodology.description: A stand that dominates only one lane still needs caveats before it earns an all-purpose top tier slot.

methodology.title: Accessibility affects value
methodology.description: Extremely punishing options can rank lower for the general player base even if specialists rate them highly.

best_for.title: Best for PvP
best_for.description: Star Platinum currently sets the sample benchmark when consistent pressure and broad matchup coverage matter most.

best_for.title: Best for PvE
best_for.description: PvE value should be judged on stable progression efficiency, not just duel strength or combo highlights.

best_for.title: Best for beginners
best_for.description: Beginners should bias toward stable progress and clean decision-making, even if that means delaying a top-tier chase.
